key_insight: SHIFT selects RLVR training data without labels or reward scores by using a single inference rollout to measure hidden-state change as a proxy for instance utility.
engineering_relevance: This gives a practical, training-free way to build compact high-value datasets for reasoning systems when labeled rewards are expensive or unavailable.
method: Run one deterministic reasoning rollout per candidate and compute the start-to-end hidden-state delta (RIRS).
Use RIRS magnitude as a quality score for each instance.
Apply quality-weighted farthest-first CoreSet selection in an RIRS-augmented feature space to preserve both utility and coverage.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports consistent gains over diversity and difficulty/uncertainty baselines across mathematical reasoning and medical QA benchmarks, especially under ultra-low selection budgets.
watch_item: RIRS depends on a specific model’s hidden-state dynamics and one rollout, so its ranking quality may be sensitive to the base model, prompt setup, and inference determinism.
paper_title: Single-Rollout Hidden-State Dynamics for Training-Free RLVR Data Selection
